extraction.one_line_summary: BALLERINI David won the Krakow sprint from never winning WorldTour before, beating in-form ACKERMANN after CAVENDISH unclipped at 1km to go.
tactical_patterns: Trek-Segafredo used classics rider STUYVEN to open sprint instead of traditional leadout, revealing team depth limitations despite dominance.
Breakaway absorbed on lap 2 of 3 at 22 seconds suggests sprint teams now calibrate perfectly for circuit finishes.
EVENEPOEL's 1:52 GC margin from 50km stage 4 attack shows Poland's parcours no longer tests WorldTour stage racers.

rider_signals.BALLERINI David: positive: First WorldTour win despite Quick-Step pedigree suggests prior misuse in support roles rather than lack of speed
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: Mechanical at 1km shows positioning still sharp but equipment handling degraded after layoff years
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: Giro points winner beaten despite being in-form reveals pure speed insufficient without positioning on technical circuits
rider_signals.STUYVEN Jasper: neutral: Classics rider opening sprint indicates Trek lacked proper leadout train despite working hardest for setup
